subtitle: Server, DB, integrations and SSL
accessDenied: No API permission for diagnostics (check groups and core_api_methods)
loadErrorBadShape: Server response has no diagnostics payload — check backend version and PHP logs
tgWaitArmed: Within 3 minutes send /start to the bot — chat and username will be saved to server config
tgWaitWebhookCleared: Bot webhook removed — updates are fetched by polling again (otherwise /start never reaches this server).
tgWaitWebhookWarnDetail: Could not reach api.telegram.org (network, proxy, firewall). Check diagnostics.telegram.proxy and access to the proxy list. Wait mode is still on — if no webhook was set, /start may still work.
tgWaitWebhookMustClear: While a webhook URL is set for the bot, updates go there—not to this long poll, so /start never arrives here. deleteWebhook must succeed (proxy/network). Details:
tgWaitWebhookDeferred: Removing the webhook happens on the next server poll (slow proxy or network can take 1–2 min). Send /start to the bot after you see the webhook-cleared message.
simulateHint: config.json has diagnostics.simulate.enabled — extra tutorial rows prefixed “[Симуляция]” are prepended to real checks (OK/warn/fail counts change). Turn off simulate.enabled in production.
tgSimAlertTitle: Send a Telegram message in the same format as a real diagnostic alert, using only simulated rows
tgSimAlertOk: Simulated diagnostic alert sent to Telegram
tgSimAlertNeedSimulate: Enable diagnostics.simulate.enabled in server config.json first
